mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, lifting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following months of positive PAYEMS growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is anchored below 0.25%, excess liquidity compresses equity risk premia and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as investors search for yield.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18% as of December 2010 vintage
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive less than 55% of the time over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and declines in unemployment (UNRATE) confirm a broad-based demand recovery validated by labor markets, jointly predicting positive S&P 500 returns via upward earnings revisions.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and UNRATE fails to add incremental explanatory power beyond individual series (F-test p > 0.05) or produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In the early phase of industrial recovery following a prolonged contraction, a month-over-month rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) predicts positive S&P 500 returns as operating leverage amplifies margins; this effect disappears late-cycle.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases month-over-month following a prior 3-month decline
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery from deep industrial contraction
failure_condition: After INDPRO rebounds from a 3-month decline, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly jumps in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) trigger rapid risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as recession probabilities spike and liquidity preferences rise.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp in a month, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative less than 55% of the time over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with a near-zero federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) creates deeply negative real interest rates, pushing capital out of cash and bonds into equities and driving positive returns.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with rising inflation
failure_condition: The joint specification of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS does not outperform single-series models (F-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window, or the interaction Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in housing starts (HOUST) lead construction activity and durable goods demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through the residential investment multiplier.
failure_condition: When HOUST rises for 3 consecutive months, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive less than 55% of the time over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals recessionary aggregate demand contraction and earnings compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as equity risk premia expand.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 QoQ growth is negative, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative less than 55% of the time over a 2-year rolling window.
